intro: Centrosolenia bryogeton is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Bryogeton or Bryogeton fern. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Centrosolenia bryogeton include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Centrosolenia bryogeton is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and salves, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
